title: Sales Revenue Forecasting - Results of a formula column to Task?
excerpt:
We want the results of a “Formula” column to show as a “Task” - Is there any way to do this? 
We seek assistance in extracting the monthly total forecast value from the Sales Ops formula column and displaying it as a rev…

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
We want the results of a “Formula” column to show as a “Task” - Is there any way to do this?

We seek assistance in extracting the monthly total forecast value from the Sales Ops formula column and displaying it as a revenue projection. We aim to create tasks labeled with the “£ total” that can appear on a separate board, with live links to update them as needed.

We want the formula outputted as a “task” so we can show visually on a dashboard how much each month the new Sales op would generate as revenue.

*numbers for example only

Sales ops Q1141×527 274 KB

Sales ops Q21178×347 59.2 KB

formula automations link-items #FinancialProjections

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml: Tagging @WLane to see if he has any advice

posts.author: WLane (Wally Lane)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Clarey, thanks for the tag. A few questions to help me understand the use case here…

  • Would the ‘task’ item on board 2 be a total from board 1 column for the month?
  • Are new items added to board 1 over the months, or are they a fixed list?
  • Is there any forecasting formula being used to project the forecast, or is it just intended to display the totals from board 1?

Thanks

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Wally,

Yes the ‘task’ item on board 2 would be a total from board 1 column for the month so if I have 10 formula columns I would want 10 tasks. Does that make sense?

The columns can be added to but for now are a fixed list.

It is just intended to display the totals from board 1.

thanks for the support!

posts.author: WLane (Wally Lane)
posts.cookedHtml: Got it, thanks. I’m on leave for 2 weeks, so I’ll ask around if someone can take a look for you in my absence.

posts.author: Ag47Panda (Steve Wasson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Clare,

Long time Monday user. First time poster on the boards here. I am a part of the ambassador program and am responding to Wally’s ask.

From what I understand you have a formula generating on Board 1 and you want that formula value to appear in Board 2 as a “task” and Board 2 is a Dashboard not a full project board correct?

You should be able to setup a Table Widget on your dashboard to see the data from Board 1 as it is and then you can filter down what data you want actually shown and it will work. Beyond that there are a ton of chart, graphs and other fun widgets that can display your sales data in a lot of different ways in the dashboard view.

If you need Board 2 to be a full project board it will be a matter of getting your raw sales data into Board 2 via manual or automated pushes. Unfortunately, formula field data cannot be traveled between boards so it would be a matter of getting the raw moved over and rebuilding the same formula. You can do much of the same view options in Board 1 as you can do in a dashboard so you might want to look at a new board view but a clean and easy dashboard is nice for quick glance big picture stuff.

Hope this helps!

Steve
